3:# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
5:# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6:# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7:# You may obtain a copy of the License at
9:#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
11:# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12:# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13:# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14:# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15:# limitations under the License.
17:# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
19:# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
21:# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22:# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23:# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24:# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25:# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26:# hardening directive set.
28:# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation.
32:# Multiple inclusion detection
36:# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition —
37:# available at source time)
39:# Canonical workload OS user name provisioned on every node by garrison.
40:# Project-wide convention; consumed by every account-relative path tinder
41:# below, substituted into BUJB_command_w via bujb_command_for_capture,
42:# and consumed by garrison/cli display strings.
43:BUJB_workload_user='bujuw_user'
45:# Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
46:BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
48:# wsl.exe invocation prefix for running a bash body as root inside the
49:# canonical workload distribution from any non-bash transport (PS heredoc,
50:# cmd.exe argv). Tinder-on-tinder so a future BUJB_wsl_distribution rename
51:# is a one-line edit. Bare wsl.exe relies on Windows PATH resolution from
52:# system32; the absolute-path form ${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} applies only in
53:# the BUJB_command_w directive where SSH-auth-time resolution requires a
54:# fully qualified path.
55:BUJB_wsl_root_bash_c="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -c"
57:# Path segment shared by every account-relative SSH state directory.
58:BUJB_path_dotssh='.ssh'
60:# authorized_keys basename — OpenSSH-defined filename. Used tinder-on-tinder
61:# in the per-coordinate-system authkeys paths and inline in garrison step4's
62:# authkeys writes.
63:BUJB_authkeys_basename='authorized_keys'
65:# Absolute path to bash on Linux/Mac/WSL — pinned to /bin/bash rather than
66:# the PATH-resolved bare 'bash' for command= directives and user-shell
67:# declarations where the absolute form is required.
68:BUJB_shell_bash='/bin/bash'
70:# POSIX stdin pseudo-device path — used by the install -D pattern in
71:# garrison step5 to plant the workload privkey atomically without any
72:# plaintext temp file on either side's disk.
73:BUJB_path_devstdin='/dev/stdin'
75:# WSL artifact basenames placed under the workload Windows profile by the
76:# garrison-w seed/import sequence.
77:BUJB_seed_basename='rbtww-seed.tar'
78:BUJB_wsl_root_basename='rbtww-fs'
80:# Cygwin install root in two slash conventions. Forward-slash form
81:# survives `command="..."` directives and cmd.exe / PS argv layers
82:# without escape pain (used in BUJB_command_c and zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin).
83:# Back-slash form is for PowerShell path arguments where Test-Path /
84:# Remove-Item expect native Windows separators (used by the obliterate
85:# Cygwin-home probe/remove). Do not collapse — the divergence is
86:# load-bearing per the layer that consumes each form.
87:BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd='C:/cygwin64'
88:BUJB_path_cygwin_root_bs="C:\\cygwin64"
90:# Windows-OpenSSH path to wsl.exe, embedded in the locked-down command=
91:# directive for shell-letter w. Forward-slash form per the same argv-layer
92:# rule as BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd.
93:BUJB_path_wsl_exe='C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe'
95:# Workload home directories per coordinate system. Tinder-on-tinder so a
96:# future BUJB_workload_user rename is a one-line edit.
97:BUJB_path_win_user_home="C:\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
98:BUJB_path_winenv_user_home="%SystemDrive%\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
99:BUJB_path_wsl_user_home="/mnt/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
100:BUJB_path_cyg_user_home="/cygdrive/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
101:BUJB_path_mac_user_home="/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
102:BUJB_path_posix_user_home="/home/${BUJB_workload_user}"
103:BUJB_path_cygwin_user_home="${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_bs}\\home\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
105:# .ssh directories under each home.
106:BUJB_path_win_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
107:BUJB_path_wsl_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_wsl_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
108:BUJB_path_cyg_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_cyg_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
109:BUJB_path_posix_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
111:# authorized_keys file under each .ssh.
112:BUJB_path_win_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_win_user_ssh}\\${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
113:BUJB_path_wsl_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_wsl_user_ssh}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
114:BUJB_path_cyg_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_cyg_user_ssh}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
116:# WSL artifacts under the Windows workload profile.
117:BUJB_path_win_seed_tarball="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_seed_basename}"
118:BUJB_path_win_wsl_root="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_wsl_root_basename}"
120:# WSL-install seed distribution: the Microsoft-published distribution that
121:# bujb_wsl_install fetches via `wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d`, exports
122:# to .tar, then re-imports under BUJB_wsl_distribution.
123:BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution='Ubuntu-24.04'
125:# Windows-side install directory housing both seed and canonical
126:# distribution VHD trees plus the intermediate .tar export.
127:BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root='C:\WSL'
129:# Windows ACL principal names used in icacls /grant arguments. The :F
130:# permission suffix is appended at use sites alongside ${BUJB_workload_user}:F
131:# so all three principals follow the same `<principal>:F` shape.
132:BUJB_acl_principal_system='SYSTEM'
133:BUJB_acl_principal_admins='BUILTIN\Administrators'
135:# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
136:# Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
137:# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
138:# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
139:# Tinder-on-tinder source-time interpolation: cygwin/wsl/distribution/user
140:# resolve at source time so the value is a fully-baked literal by the time
141:# bujb_command_for_capture echoes it. \"...\" pairs are literal escaped
142:# quotes the remote sshd parser expects around the command directive's
143:# argument; \$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND defers expansion to the remote shell.
144:BUJB_command_b="command=\"${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
145:BUJB_command_c="command=\"${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd}/bin/bash --login -c \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
146:BUJB_command_w="command=\"${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec ${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"
148:# Workload privkey destination path on the remote (relative to the workload
149:# account home directory). Uniform across shell-letters under the current
150:# design — if a future shell needs a divergent keypath, fan out per-letter.
151:BUJB_workload_keypath='.ssh/id_ed25519'
153:# Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
154:# fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
155:# bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.

19:# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
21:# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22:# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23:# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24:# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25:# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26:# hardening directive set.
28:# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation.
32:# Multiple inclusion detection
36:# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition —
37:# available at source time)
39:# Canonical workload OS user name provisioned on every node by garrison.
40:# Project-wide convention; consumed by every account-relative path tinder
41:# below, substituted into BUJB_command_w via bujb_command_for_capture,
42:# and consumed by garrison/cli display strings.
43:BUJB_workload_user='bujuw_user'
45:# Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
46:BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
48:# wsl.exe invocation prefix for running a bash body as root inside the
49:# canonical workload distribution from any non-bash transport (PS heredoc,
50:# cmd.exe argv). Tinder-on-tinder so a future BUJB_wsl_distribution rename
51:# is a one-line edit. Bare wsl.exe relies on Windows PATH resolution from
52:# system32; the absolute-path form ${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} applies only in
53:# the BUJB_command_w directive where SSH-auth-time resolution requires a
54:# fully qualified path.
55:BUJB_wsl_root_bash_c="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -c"
57:# Path segment shared by every account-relative SSH state directory.
58:BUJB_path_dotssh='.ssh'
60:# authorized_keys basename — OpenSSH-defined filename. Used tinder-on-tinder
61:# in the per-coordinate-system authkeys paths and inline in garrison step4's
62:# authkeys writes.
63:BUJB_authkeys_basename='authorized_keys'
65:# Absolute path to bash on Linux/Mac/WSL — pinned to /bin/bash rather than
66:# the PATH-resolved bare 'bash' for command= directives and user-shell
67:# declarations where the absolute form is required.
68:BUJB_shell_bash='/bin/bash'
70:# POSIX stdin pseudo-device path — used by the install -D pattern in
71:# garrison step5 to plant the workload privkey atomically without any
72:# plaintext temp file on either side's disk.
73:BUJB_path_devstdin='/dev/stdin'
75:# WSL artifact basenames placed under the workload Windows profile by the
76:# garrison-w seed/import sequence.
77:BUJB_seed_basename='rbtww-seed.tar'
78:BUJB_wsl_root_basename='rbtww-fs'
80:# Cygwin install root in two slash conventions. Forward-slash form
81:# survives `command="..."` directives and cmd.exe / PS argv layers
82:# without escape pain (used in BUJB_command_c and zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin).
83:# Back-slash form is for PowerShell path arguments where Test-Path /
84:# Remove-Item expect native Windows separators (used by the obliterate
85:# Cygwin-home probe/remove). Do not collapse — the divergence is
86:# load-bearing per the layer that consumes each form.
87:BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd='C:/cygwin64'
88:BUJB_path_cygwin_root_bs="C:\\cygwin64"
90:# Windows-OpenSSH path to wsl.exe, embedded in the locked-down command=
91:# directive for shell-letter w. Forward-slash form per the same argv-layer
92:# rule as BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd.
93:BUJB_path_wsl_exe='C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe'
95:# Workload home directories per coordinate system. Tinder-on-tinder so a
96:# future BUJB_workload_user rename is a one-line edit.
97:BUJB_path_win_user_home="C:\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
98:BUJB_path_winenv_user_home="%SystemDrive%\\Users\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
99:BUJB_path_wsl_user_home="/mnt/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
100:BUJB_path_cyg_user_home="/cygdrive/c/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
101:BUJB_path_mac_user_home="/Users/${BUJB_workload_user}"
102:BUJB_path_posix_user_home="/home/${BUJB_workload_user}"
103:BUJB_path_cygwin_user_home="${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_bs}\\home\\${BUJB_workload_user}"
105:# .ssh directories under each home.
106:BUJB_path_win_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
107:BUJB_path_wsl_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_wsl_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
108:BUJB_path_cyg_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_cyg_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
109:BUJB_path_posix_user_ssh="${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}/${BUJB_path_dotssh}"
111:# authorized_keys file under each .ssh.
112:BUJB_path_win_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_win_user_ssh}\\${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
113:BUJB_path_wsl_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_wsl_user_ssh}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
114:BUJB_path_cyg_user_authkeys="${BUJB_path_cyg_user_ssh}/${BUJB_authkeys_basename}"
116:# WSL artifacts under the Windows workload profile.
117:BUJB_path_win_seed_tarball="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_seed_basename}"
118:BUJB_path_win_wsl_root="${BUJB_path_win_user_home}\\${BUJB_wsl_root_basename}"
120:# WSL-install seed distribution: the Microsoft-published distribution that
121:# bujb_wsl_install fetches via `wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d`, exports
122:# to .tar, then re-imports under BUJB_wsl_distribution.
123:BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution='Ubuntu-24.04'
125:# Windows-side install directory housing both seed and canonical
126:# distribution VHD trees plus the intermediate .tar export.
127:BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root='C:\WSL'
129:# Windows ACL principal names used in icacls /grant arguments. The :F
130:# permission suffix is appended at use sites alongside ${BUJB_workload_user}:F
131:# so all three principals follow the same `<principal>:F` shape.
132:BUJB_acl_principal_system='SYSTEM'
133:BUJB_acl_principal_admins='BUILTIN\Administrators'
135:# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
136:# Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
137:# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
138:# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.

142:# quotes the remote sshd parser expects around the command directive's

146:BUJB_command_w="command=\"${BUJB_path_wsl_exe} --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user ${BUJB_workload_user} --exec ${BUJB_shell_bash} -lc \\\"\$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\\\"\",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding"

387	bujb_resolve_investiture() {

402	# bujb_command_for_capture SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
403	# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w. _capture form so callers
404	# may use `$()` per BCG line 502; returns 1 on invalid letter, callers must

407	bujb_command_for_capture() {

467	}
468	
469	# zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl} STMT -- run a single bash statement as
470	# the privileged user on the remote node, in the named shell environment.
471	# Variant functions per platform (no cryptic letter parameter): the call
472	# site picks the shell by name, and the function selects the matching
473	# remote invoker (`bash`, cygwin's bash --login, or wsl.exe ... bash).
474	# Embedded " characters are escaped to \" so the outer "..." of
475	# `bash -c "..."` survives the cmd.exe / Windows argv-parser layer
476	# (relevant for cygwin/wsl variants via Windows OpenSSH).
477	#
478	# Single-statement contract per WSG SH-10: STMT must be exactly one of
479	# (1) one simple command with arguments and redirections, (2) one pipeline
480	# optionally preceded by `set -o pipefail`, or (3) one simple command
481	# whose argument is itself an inner-shell body. Multi-statement work
482	# decomposes into multiple round-trips per the Capture-Decide-Dispatch
483	# pattern; bash on the curia owns the state machine.
484	#
485	# Caller may pipe stdin into any variant (e.g.
486	#   `zbujb_admin_exec_native "install ... /dev/stdin '<target>'" < <file>`)

488	# forwards it to the remote command's FD 0, the body's single command
489	# reads from /dev/stdin. Used by garrison step5 to plant the workload
490	# key without ever materializing the plaintext on either side's disk.
491	#
492	# Caller redirects stdout/stderr for capture; returns ssh exit code.
493	# These functions do NOT transform $. Body authors are responsible for
494	# per-shell $-escape discipline (see WSG SH-6: only the wsl variant's
495	# bodies need `\$name` escaping).
496	zbujb_admin_exec_native() {
497	  zbujb_admin_exec_impl 'bash' "$@"
498	}
499	
500	zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin() {
501	  zbujb_admin_exec_impl "${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd}/bin/bash --login" "$@"
502	}
503	
504	zbujb_admin_exec_wsl() {
505	  zbujb_admin_exec_impl "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash" "$@"
506	}
507	
508	# zbujb_admin_exec_impl REMOTE_INVOKER STMT -- shared body for the three
509	# zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl} variants. Not called directly by
510	# step code; callers always go through one of the named variants so the
511	# call site declares its target shell.
512	zbujb_admin_exec_impl() {
513	  zbujb_sentinel
514	  local -r z_remote_invoker="${1:-}"
515	  test -n "${z_remote_invoker}" \
516	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_exec_impl: REMOTE_INVOKER required (call via zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl})"
517	  shift
518	  test $# -eq 1 \
519	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_exec_*: requires exactly one statement (got $#); decompose multi-statement work via Capture-Decide-Dispatch per WSG SH-10"
520	  local -r z_body="$1"
521	
522	  # Escape " → \" for the cmd.exe / Windows argv-parser layer.
523	  local -r z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"
524	
525	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
526	      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
527	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
528	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
529	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
530	      "${z_remote_invoker} -c \"${z_body_escaped}\""
531	}
532	
533	# zbujb_admin_powershell BODY... -- run a PowerShell statement chain on
534	# the remote node as the privileged user. Prepends discipline prelude:
535	# $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' (PS errors terminate), $env:WSL_UTF8=1
536	# (wsl.exe emits UTF-8), $LASTEXITCODE=0 (initialize so the trailing
537	# if-check doesn't trip on the $null default — $null -ne 0 evaluates True
538	# in PowerShell typed comparison, which would fire `exit $null` and
539	# discard buffered object-formatter output for cmdlets like Get-LocalUser
540	# whose tables haven't flushed to stdout yet). Trailing if-check
541	# propagates $LASTEXITCODE on non-zero from native commands in the body.
542	# Caller redirects stdout/stderr; returns ssh's exit code.
543	zbujb_admin_powershell() {
544	  zbujb_sentinel
545	  test $# -ge 1 \
546	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: PowerShell command body required"
547	  local -r z_body="$*"
548	
549	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
550	      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
551	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
552	      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
553	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
554	      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\""
555	}

bujb_resolve_investiture() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  zburc_sentinel
  zburn_sentinel
  zburp_sentinel

  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"

  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE" priv
  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"   work

  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
}

# bujb_command_for_capture SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w. _capture form so callers
# may use `$()` per BCG line 502; returns 1 on invalid letter, callers must
# `|| buc_die`. All tinder-on-tinder interpolation already resolved at
# source time — uniform letter-case, no per-letter substitution gymnastics.
bujb_command_for_capture() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison helpers

zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|c|w) ;;
    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BURN_PLATFORM
# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c|w)
      test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
      ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_workload_home_capture LETTER -- emit the absolute workload home path
# on the remote node for the given shell-letter. _capture form per BCG line
# 502; returns 1 on unsupported platform or invalid letter, callers must
# `|| buc_die`.
zbujb_workload_home_capture() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux) echo "${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}" ;;
        bubep_mac)   echo "${BUJB_path_mac_user_home}"   ;;
        *)           return 1 ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c|w) echo "${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}" ;;
    *)   return 1 ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl} STMT -- run a single bash statement as
# the privileged user on the remote node, in the named shell environment.
# Variant functions per platform (no cryptic letter parameter): the call
# site picks the shell by name, and the function selects the matching
# remote invoker (`bash`, cygwin's bash --login, or wsl.exe ... bash).
# Embedded " characters are escaped to \" so the outer "..." of
# `bash -c "..."` survives the cmd.exe / Windows argv-parser layer
# (relevant for cygwin/wsl variants via Windows OpenSSH).
#
# Single-statement contract per WSG SH-10: STMT must be exactly one of
# (1) one simple command with arguments and redirections, (2) one pipeline
# optionally preceded by `set -o pipefail`, or (3) one simple command
# whose argument is itself an inner-shell body. Multi-statement work
# decomposes into multiple round-trips per the Capture-Decide-Dispatch
# pattern; bash on the curia owns the state machine.
#
# Caller may pipe stdin into any variant (e.g.
#   `zbujb_admin_exec_native "install ... /dev/stdin '<target>'" < <file>`)
# — bash redirection attaches the file to FD 0, ssh inherits it, sshd
# forwards it to the remote command's FD 0, the body's single command
# reads from /dev/stdin. Used by garrison step5 to plant the workload
# key without ever materializing the plaintext on either side's disk.
#
# Caller redirects stdout/stderr for capture; returns ssh exit code.
# These functions do NOT transform $. Body authors are responsible for
# per-shell $-escape discipline (see WSG SH-6: only the wsl variant's
# bodies need `\$name` escaping).
zbujb_admin_exec_native() {
  zbujb_admin_exec_impl 'bash' "$@"
}

zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin() {
  zbujb_admin_exec_impl "${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd}/bin/bash --login" "$@"
}

zbujb_admin_exec_wsl() {
  zbujb_admin_exec_impl "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash" "$@"
}

# zbujb_admin_exec_impl REMOTE_INVOKER STMT -- shared body for the three
# zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl} variants. Not called directly by
# step code; callers always go through one of the named variants so the
# call site declares its target shell.
zbujb_admin_exec_impl() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_remote_invoker="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_remote_invoker}" \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_exec_impl: REMOTE_INVOKER required (call via zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl})"
  shift
  test $# -eq 1 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_exec_*: requires exactly one statement (got $#); decompose multi-statement work via Capture-Decide-Dispatch per WSG SH-10"
  local -r z_body="$1"

  # Escape " → \" for the cmd.exe / Windows argv-parser layer.
  local -r z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
      "${z_remote_invoker} -c \"${z_body_escaped}\""
}

# zbujb_admin_powershell BODY... -- run a PowerShell statement chain on
# the remote node as the privileged user. Prepends discipline prelude:
# $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' (PS errors terminate), $env:WSL_UTF8=1
# (wsl.exe emits UTF-8), $LASTEXITCODE=0 (initialize so the trailing
# if-check doesn't trip on the $null default — $null -ne 0 evaluates True
# in PowerShell typed comparison, which would fire `exit $null` and
# discard buffered object-formatter output for cmdlets like Get-LocalUser
# whose tables haven't flushed to stdout yet). Trailing if-check
# propagates $LASTEXITCODE on non-zero from native commands in the body.
# Caller redirects stdout/stderr; returns ssh's exit code.
zbujb_admin_powershell() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_powershell: PowerShell command body required"
  local -r z_body="$*"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"        \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"             \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"      \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"  \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"  \
      "${BUJB_ps_invoke_command} \"${BUJB_ps_prelude} ${z_body}; if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit \$LASTEXITCODE }\""
}

bujb_fenestrate() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform

  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2

  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Public: WSL Install (provision canonical WSL distribution)

zbujb_wsl_install_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "wsl-install requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
}

# bujb_wsl_install -- idempotently provision BUJB_wsl_distribution by purging
# any prior state, installing an Ubuntu-24.04 seed, exporting it to a .tar,
# importing under the canonical name, then unregistering the seed and removing
# the .tar. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
# Each step propagates failure via zbujb_admin_powershell + || buc_die.
bujb_wsl_install() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_wsl_install: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_wsl_install_assert_platform

  local -r z_distro_dir="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  local -r z_tar_path="${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}\\${BUJB_wsl_distribution}.tar"

  buc_step "WSL Install: ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} on ${BURN_HOST} (seed: ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution})"

  buc_step "  [1/6] Purge prior state (idempotent: unregister both, remove tar+dir)"
  local z_wsl_list=""
  z_wsl_list=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "wsl.exe --list --quiet") \
    || z_wsl_list=""
  if grep -qFx "${BUJB_wsl_distribution}" <<<"${z_wsl_list}"; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to unregister prior ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"
  fi
  if grep -qFx "${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" <<<"${z_wsl_list}"; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to unregister prior ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  fi
  local z_tar_present z_dir_present
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  z_dir_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_distro_dir}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_distro_dir}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove prior ${z_tar_path}"
  fi
  if [[ "${z_dir_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '${z_distro_dir}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove prior ${z_distro_dir}"
  fi

  buc_step "  [2/6] Ensure ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root} directory"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path '${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}' -Force | Out-Null" \
    || buc_die "Failed to create ${BUJB_path_win_wsl_install_root}"

  buc_step "  [3/6] Install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed distribution"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --install --no-launch -d ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to install ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} seed"

  buc_step "  [4/6] Export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} to ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to export ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [5/6] Import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} from ${z_tar_path}"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} '${z_distro_dir}' '${z_tar_path}'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to import ${BUJB_wsl_distribution}"

  buc_step "  [6/6] Cleanup: unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution} and remove .tar"
  zbujb_admin_powershell "wsl.exe --unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to unregister ${BUJB_wsl_seed_distribution}"
  z_tar_present=$(zbujb_powershell_capture zbujb_privileged "Test-Path '${z_tar_path}'") \
    || buc_die "Failed to probe ${z_tar_path}"
  if [[ "${z_tar_present}" == "True" ]]; then
    zbujb_admin_powershell "Remove-Item -Force '${z_tar_path}'" \
      || buc_die "Failed to remove ${z_tar_path}"
  fi

  buc_step "WSL Install succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Privileged SSH (thin admin-side pass-through)

# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
# ssh's exit code.
bujb_privileged_ssh() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
  test $# -ge 1 \
    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: command required"

  buc_step "Privileged SSH: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"           \
      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes}"         \
      -o "${BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15}"     \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"     \
      "$@"                                       \
    || z_exit=$?
  return "${z_exit}"
}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
20	#
21	# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
22	# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
23	# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
24	# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Command Functions
32	
33	# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
34	# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
35	bujb_resolve() {
36	  zbujb_sentinel
37	  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
38	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
39	
40	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
41	
42	  bujb_resolve_investiture
43	
44	  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
45	  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
46	  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
47	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
48	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
49	  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
50	  buc_bare "  BUJB_workload_user      = ${BUJB_workload_user}"
51	}
52	
53	# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
54	bujb_knock() {
55	  zbujb_sentinel
56	  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
57	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
58	
59	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
60	
61	  bujb_resolve_investiture
62	
63	  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"
64	
65	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
66	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
67	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
68	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
69	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
70	      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
71	      true                                        \
72	    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUZ_FOLIO}"
73	
74	  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
75	}
76	
77	# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
78	# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
79	# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
80	# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
81	bujb_command_file() {
82	  zbujb_sentinel
83	  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
84	  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
85	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
86	
87	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
88	
89	  local -r z_command_file="${1:-}"
90	  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
91	  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
92	  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"
93	
94	  bujb_resolve_investiture
95	
96	  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"
97	  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"
98	
99	  local z_exit=0
100	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
101	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
102	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
103	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
104	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
105	      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
106	      'bash -s'                                   \
107	      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
108	      > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDOUT}"                  \
109	      2> "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"                 \
110	    || z_exit=$?
111	
112	  echo "${z_exit}" > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}" \
113	    || buc_die "Failed to write exit-code capture: ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}"
114	
115	  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
116	    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"
117	
118	  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
119	  buc_step "Capture complete"
120	}
121	
122	# bujb_fenestrate - fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only).
123	bujb_fenestrate_command() {
124	  zbujb_sentinel
125	  buc_doc_brief "Fenestrate — admin SSH key trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
126	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
127	
128	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
129	  bujb_resolve_investiture
130	  bujb_fenestrate
131	}
132	
133	# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
134	# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
135	bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
136	  zbujb_sentinel
137	  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
138	  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
139	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
140	
141	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
142	  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die
143	
144	  bujb_resolve_investiture
145	  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
146	}
147	
148	# bujb_wsl_install_command - idempotently provision the canonical WSL distro.
149	bujb_wsl_install_command() {
150	  zbujb_sentinel
151	  buc_doc_brief "WSL Install — provision canonical WSL distribution from Ubuntu-24.04 seed (Windows, idempotent)"
152	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
153	
154	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
155	  bujb_resolve_investiture
156	  bujb_wsl_install
157	}
158	
159	# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
160	bujb_garrison_bash() {
161	  zbujb_sentinel
162	  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
163	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
164	
165	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
166	  bujb_resolve_investiture
167	  bujb_garrison b
168	}
169	
170	# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
171	bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
172	  zbujb_sentinel
173	  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
174	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
175	
176	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
177	  bujb_resolve_investiture
178	  bujb_garrison c
179	}
180	
181	# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
182	bujb_garrison_wsl() {
183	  zbujb_sentinel
184	  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
185	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
186	
187	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
188	  bujb_resolve_investiture
189	  bujb_garrison w
190	}
191	
192	# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
193	bujb_interactive_session() {
194	  zbujb_sentinel
195	  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
196	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
197	
198	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
199	
200	  bujb_resolve_investiture
201	
202	  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"
203	
204	  exec ssh -t                                     \
205	      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
206	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
207	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
208	      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
209	      "bash -i"
210	}
211	
212	######################################################################
213	# Furnish and Main
214	
215	zbujb_furnish() {
216	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
217	  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
218	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
219	
220	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
221	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
222	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
223	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
224	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
225	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
226	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
227	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burp_regime.sh"
228	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
229	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
230	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
231	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
232	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwz_zipper.sh"
233	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"
234	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujp_preflight.sh"
235	
236	  zbuv_kindle
237	  zburd_kindle
238	  zburd_enforce
239	
240	  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"
241	
242	  zburc_kindle
243	  zburc_enforce
244	
245	  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"
246	
247	  zburs_kindle
248	  zburs_enforce
249	
250	  zbupr_kindle
251	  zbuz_kindle
252	  zbuwz_kindle
253	  zbujb_kindle
254	  zbujp_kindle
255	
256	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
257	
258	  local -r z_burp_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burp.env"
259	  test -f "${z_burp_file}" || buc_die "BURP profile not found: ${z_burp_file}"
260	  source "${z_burp_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURP: ${z_burp_file}"
261	  zburp_kindle
262	  zburp_enforce
263	
264	  # Cross-reference: investiture name (BUZ_FOLIO) must equal a registered investiture.
265	  # The 1:1 constraint is enforced by file presence — the BURN profile directory
266	  # name IS the investiture, and it must match BUZ_FOLIO exactly.
267	  local -r z_burn_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
268	  test -f "${z_burn_file}" || buc_die "Investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO} has no matching investiture (BURN profile not found): ${z_burn_file}"
269	  source "${z_burn_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_burn_file}"
270	  zburn_kindle
271	  zburn_enforce
272	}
273	
274	buc_execute bujb_ "Bash Utility Jurisdiction" zbujb_furnish "$@"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
#
# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
bujb_resolve() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_workload_user      = ${BUJB_workload_user}"
}

# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
bujb_knock() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUZ_FOLIO}"

  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
}

# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
bujb_command_file() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_command_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      'bash -s'                                   \
      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
      > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDOUT}"                  \
      2> "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"                 \
    || z_exit=$?

  echo "${z_exit}" > "${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write exit-code capture: ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_EXITCODE}"

  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${ZBUJB_OUTPUT_STDERR}"

  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
  buc_step "Capture complete"
}

# bujb_fenestrate - fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only).
bujb_fenestrate_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Fenestrate — admin SSH key trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_fenestrate
}

# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die

  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
}

# bujb_wsl_install_command - idempotently provision the canonical WSL distro.
bujb_wsl_install_command() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "WSL Install — provision canonical WSL distribution from Ubuntu-24.04 seed (Windows, idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_wsl_install
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  exec ssh -t                                     \
      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "bash -i"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zbujb_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burp_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwz_zipper.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujp_preflight.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle
  zbuz_kindle
  zbuwz_kindle
  zbujb_kindle
  zbujp_kindle

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_burp_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burp.env"
  test -f "${z_burp_file}" || buc_die "BURP profile not found: ${z_burp_file}"
  source "${z_burp_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURP: ${z_burp_file}"
  zburp_kindle
  zburp_enforce

  # Cross-reference: investiture name (BUZ_FOLIO) must equal a registered investiture.
  # The 1:1 constraint is enforced by file presence — the BURN profile directory
  # name IS the investiture, and it must match BUZ_FOLIO exactly.
  local -r z_burn_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_burn_file}" || buc_die "Investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO} has no matching investiture (BURN profile not found): ${z_burn_file}"
  source "${z_burn_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_burn_file}"
  zburn_kindle
  zburn_enforce
}

buc_execute bujb_ "Bash Utility Jurisdiction" zbujb_furnish "$@"

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion guard
24	test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
25	ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Colophon registry initialization
29	
30	zbuwz_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"
32	
33	  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
34	  zbuz_sentinel
35	
36	  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
37	  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
38	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
39	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
40	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
41	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
42	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
43	  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"
44	
45	  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
46	  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
47	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
48	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"
49	
50	  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
51	  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
52	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
53	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"
54	
55	  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
56	  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
57	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
58	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"
59	
60	  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
61	  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
62	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
63	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
64	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"
65	
66	  # Privileged Regime subsystem (burp_cli.sh)
67	  z_mod="burp_cli.sh"
68	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_VALIDATE "buw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "burp_validate" "param1" "Validate BURP profile"
69	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_RENDER   "buw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "burp_render"   "param1" "Render BURP profile"
70	  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_LIST     "buw-rpl" "${z_mod}" "burp_list"     ""       "List BURP profiles"
71	
72	  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
73	  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
74	  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"
75	
76	  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
77	  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
78	  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"
79	
80	  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
81	  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
82	  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"
83	
84	  # Handbook — jurisdiction (buhj_cli.sh)
85	  z_mod="buhj_cli.sh"
86	  buz_enroll BUWZ_HJ0_TOP           "buw-hj0"   "${z_mod}" "buhj_top"                ""        "Jurisdiction handbook landing + admin SSH bootstrap"
87	
88	  # Jurisdiction operational — workload ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
89	  z_mod="bujb_cli.sh"
90	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_KNOCK          "buw-jwk"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_knock"               "param1"  "Knock — probe workload SSH reachability"
91	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_COMMAND_FILE   "buw-jwc"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_command_file"        "param1"  "Run command file as workload, capture outputs"
92	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_INTERACTIVE    "buw-jws"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_interactive_session" "param1"  "Interactive SSH session as workload"
93	
94	  # Jurisdiction operational — fenestrate (bujb_cli.sh)
95	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_FENESTRATE       "buw-jpF"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_fenestrate_command" "param1"  "Fenestrate — admin SSH trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
96	
97	  # Jurisdiction operational — privileged SSH pass-through (bujb_cli.sh)
98	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_PRIVILEGED_SSH   "buw-jpS"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_privileged_ssh_command" "param1"  "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
99	
100	  # Jurisdiction operational — WSL install (bujb_cli.sh, Windows only)
101	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_WSL_INSTALL      "buw-jpW"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_wsl_install_command"    "param1"  "WSL Install — provision canonical WSL distribution from Ubuntu-24.04 seed (Windows, idempotent)"
102	
103	  # Jurisdiction operational — garrison ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
104	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_BASH    "buw-jpGb"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_bash"     "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter b: native bash, Linux/Mac)"
105	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_CYGWIN  "buw-jpGc"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_cygwin"   "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter c: Cygwin bash, Windows)"
106	  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_WSL     "buw-jpGw"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_wsl"      "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter w: WSL bash, Windows)"
107	
108	  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
109	}
110	
111	######################################################################
112	# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)
113	
114	zbuwz_healthcheck() {
115	  zbuwz_sentinel
116	  buz_healthcheck
117	}
118	
119	######################################################################
120	# Internal sentinel
121	
122	zbuwz_sentinel() {
123	  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
124	}
125	
126	# eof
127

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#

# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#

#

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)

  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)

  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"

  # Privileged Regime subsystem (burp_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_VALIDATE "buw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "burp_validate" "param1" "Validate BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_RENDER   "buw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "burp_render"   "param1" "Render BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_LIST     "buw-rpl" "${z_mod}" "burp_list"     ""       "List BURP profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Handbook — jurisdiction (buhj_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhj_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HJ0_TOP           "buw-hj0"   "${z_mod}" "buhj_top"                ""        "Jurisdiction handbook landing + admin SSH bootstrap"

  # Jurisdiction operational — workload ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bujb_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_KNOCK          "buw-jwk"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_knock"               "param1"  "Knock — probe workload SSH reachability"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_COMMAND_FILE   "buw-jwc"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_command_file"        "param1"  "Run command file as workload, capture outputs"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_INTERACTIVE    "buw-jws"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_interactive_session" "param1"  "Interactive SSH session as workload"

  # Jurisdiction operational — fenestrate (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_FENESTRATE       "buw-jpF"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_fenestrate_command" "param1"  "Fenestrate — admin SSH trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"

  # Jurisdiction operational — privileged SSH pass-through (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_PRIVILEGED_SSH   "buw-jpS"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_privileged_ssh_command" "param1"  "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"

  # Jurisdiction operational — WSL install (bujb_cli.sh, Windows only)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_WSL_INSTALL      "buw-jpW"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_wsl_install_command"    "param1"  "WSL Install — provision canonical WSL distribution from Ubuntu-24.04 seed (Windows, idempotent)"

  # Jurisdiction operational — garrison ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_BASH    "buw-jpGb"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_bash"     "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter b: native bash, Linux/Mac)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_CYGWIN  "buw-jpGc"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_cygwin"   "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter c: Cygwin bash, Windows)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_WSL     "buw-jpGw"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_wsl"      "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter w: WSL bash, Windows)"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

file.content:
bujb_garrison() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  local -r z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUZ_FOLIO} (${BURN_HOST})"

  bujp_preflight                     "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_obliterate_workload
  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    w)
      # Three-namespace garrison-w (BUSJGW): the workload owns its own
      # WSL distribution. Privileged orchestrator conducts; workload's
      # SSH logon session executes the per-user-HKCU work.
      zbujb_garrison_w_export_seed
      zbujb_garrison_w_init_wsl
      zbujb_garrison_w_lockdown
      zbujb_garrison_w_seed_cleanup
      ;;
    *)
      zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key   "${z_letter}"
      ;;
  esac
  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Fenestrate helpers (BUSJPF — Windows OpenSSH only)
#
# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.

zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
}

input.description: Check if sudo/admin-group appear in BUSJI specs

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

content.text:
Before writing code, I hit a real ambiguity in the docket vs the locked specs that materially changes scope.

**The tension.** The docket Boundary line names three probes migrating into invigilate: "(sudo NOPASSWD, admin-group, WSL distribution) move under the invigilate verb that owns them." The Character line locks BUSJI{W,M,L} as the authority. But searching the three specs returns no `sudo`, `admin.group`, or `nopasswd` hits — only WSL distribution actually appears as an invigilate fact (step 7 of BUSJIW). Sudo NOPASSWD and admin-group membership are documented in the paddock Standing Notes as "operator-managed precondition" facts, but the locked spec doesn't list them.

**The Done-when target** says "zero inline posture probes remain" in bujp_preflight, which reads cleanly only if all three migrate.

**Two paths:**

- **A. Honor locked specs.** Only `zbujp_probe_wsl_distribution` migrates into `bujb_invigilate_windows`. `zbujp_probe_sudo` and `zbujp_probe_admin_group_mac` stay in bujp_preflight as garrison workload-precondition probes (privilege-grant, not host-posture). Re-read "zero inline posture probes remain" as "zero host-posture probes remain" — privilege-grant is a separate category.
- **B. Honor docket directive.** Add sudo NOPASSWD to invigilate-{linux,macos} and admin-group to invigilate-macos, exceeding the locked spec. Treats sudo/admin-group as operator-managed posture facts of the same kind as `openssh-server installed` (which the locked Linux spec does treat as a fact).

Which path?

message.content: Help me choose a simple but workable direction here.

content.text:
**Recommend Path A** — honor the locked specs. Three reasons:

1. **The Character line is unambiguous.** "BUSJI{W,M,L} are locked" is the explicit instruction; the Boundary line in the same docket is older language describing the migration model. When two lines conflict, the locked-spec line wins — that's what "locked" means.

2. **Spec churn during implementation is the worst time.** ₢A-AA6→AA9 just built that spec content four paces ago. Editing it now to add facts means the implementation and the spec drift in the same commit, with nothing to verify the implementation against. Path B would force a spec-edit pace inside an implementation pace.

3. **The residual category is real, not a fudge.** Sudo NOPASSWD and admin-group are operator-privilege grants on the SSH-admin account — what the admin SSH user is authorized to do. Invigilate is host posture — what the machine is configured as. These ARE different categories; the paddock Standing Notes treats sudo as "operator-managed precondition" not "host posture." Path A keeps each probe with the category that actually fits.

**What this looks like concretely:**

- `bujb_invigilate_{windows,macos,linux}` implements exactly the BUSJI{W,M,L} specs — nothing more.
- `bujp_preflight` becomes: invigilate-{platform} call → then for letters b/c/w, retain the remaining workload-precondition probes (sudo for b on linux/mac, sudo+admin-group for b on mac, none for c, none for w since WSL-distribution-presence migrated). The `zbujp_probe_ssh_connect` per-letter probe stays — it's workload-shell-environment reachability, which neither invigilate nor sudo cover.
- I'll flag a candidate itch in the wrap summary: "If sudo NOPASSWD and admin-group should be BUSJI facts rather than bujp probes, extend the locked specs in a follow-on pace."

This keeps the pace mechanical and the spec stable. Proceed with Path A?

message.content: Do you think this will work?  Or are the specs wrong?

content.text:
Honest answer: **the specs are incomplete, but Path A still works for this pace.**

**Where the specs are wrong.** The paddock says invigilate is "Single source of truth for 'correctly-configured' per platform... garrison's precondition check." That promise requires invigilate to cover everything garrison precondition-needs. Garrison uses `sudo -n` (userdel/useradd/install on linux+mac) and admin-group on mac. The Standing Notes call these "operator-managed precondition." That's exactly the shape of a handbook-scope invigilate fact — yet BUSJIL/BUSJIM don't list them. The locked specs forgot two operator-managed facts per their own concept.

**Why Path A still works anyway.** What's missing from the specs is *additive* — facts that aren't there don't break the facts that are there. Implementing BUSJI{W,M,L} as locked produces three working invigilate verbs that correctly verify the facts they cover. The gap is that garrison's full precondition becomes "invigilate-{platform} AND the residual bujp probes," not "invigilate-{platform} alone." That's an aesthetic/architectural smell, not a correctness bug.

**The clean recovery shape.** A follow-on pace — call it something like `extend-busji-with-operator-precondition-facts` — does three things together:
1. Adds sudo NOPASSWD to BUSJIL + BUSJIM with handbook-scope failure pointer.
2. Adds admin-group to BUSJIM with handbook-scope failure pointer.
3. Migrates `zbujp_probe_sudo` + `zbujp_probe_admin_group_mac` into the invigilate verbs, simplifying `zbujp_diag_sudo_missing` from its current elaborate scp+visudo-cf snippet-staging down to a fact-named diagnostic with handbook pointer (matching the other invigilate facts).

That last simplification is worth its own pace — the current sudoers snippet generation is recovery-oriented, not verification-oriented. Invigilate's spec shape is pure assertion. Squashing the elaborate diagnostic into a pure pointer changes posture meaningfully.

**My recommendation.** Proceed Path A for this pace as the mechanical translation it claims to be. Mint an itch in the wrap summary describing the spec gap and the follow-on pace shape. The operator (you) decides whether to slate it next or let it sit.

Proceed?

input.content:
# jjezs_slate extend-busji-operator-precondition-facts

## Character
Spec edit + small implementation migration.

## Goal
Close the spec gap surfaced during ₢A-ABD: BUSJIL/BUSJIM omit operator-managed
precondition facts (sudo NOPASSWD; admin-group on mac). Extend each spec with
handbook-scope facts in the existing step pattern. Migrate the corresponding
probes from bujp_preflight into the invigilate verbs.

## Boundary
- Mirror existing BUSJI{L,M} step shape (`busc_call`/`busc_store`/`busc_require`/
  `busc_fatal`); no new operation kinds.
- Simplify bujp_preflight's current scp+visudo-cf snippet-staging diagnostic to
  a handbook-pointer fatal — recovery copy belongs in BUSJHL/BUSJHM.
- After this pace, bujp_preflight is pure verb-delegation: invigilate-{platform}
  + workload-shell reachability probe (the per-letter reachability check is
  not host-posture, retained).

## Done when
- Specs extended; handbook-render fixture green.
- bujp_preflight devoid of inline posture probes.
- bash -n; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh.

192	# powershell_capture:
193	#   $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'  — PS errors terminate the session.
194	#   $env:WSL_UTF8 = 1                — wsl.exe emits UTF-8 (not UTF-16).
195	#   $LASTEXITCODE = 0                — initialize so a trailing if-check
196	#     does not trip on the $null default ($null -ne 0 evaluates True in
197	#     PowerShell typed comparison, which would fire `exit $null` and
198	#     discard buffered object-formatter output for cmdlets like
199	#     Get-LocalUser whose tables have not flushed to stdout yet).
200	BUJB_ps_prelude="\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \$env:WSL_UTF8 = 1; \$LASTEXITCODE = 0;"
